'''Matty Blythe''' (born 20 November 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]] (two spells) and the [[Bradford Bulls]], and in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for the [[Leigh Centurions]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), the [[Bradford Bulls]], and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] ([[Championship (rugby league)#Dual registration|dual registration]]), as a {{rlp|wg|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Blythe's career began with the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] club in 2007.

==Bradford Bulls==
In 2013 Blythe was loaned out to [[Super League]] side the [[Bradford Bulls]] for the [[2013 Bradford Bulls season]]. Blythe played in the pre-season friendlies against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

The following year, he featured in six consecutive games from the commencement of the season. He then missed a large chunk of games through injury. Blythe returned and featured in Round 14 Leeds to Round 21 Wigan and also in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[London Broncos]]. Blythe scored seven tries in the regular season that year, which included a brace against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]. He signed a permanent three-year deal with the Bradford club towards the back end of the [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014season]].

Blythe missed Rounds 1–5 due to injury. He featured in Round 6 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 9 Leeds. He was injured for Rounds 10–14. Blythe played in Round 15 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) to Round 20 ([[St Helens R.F.C.]]) then in Round 22 Wigan to Round 26 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]). He also featured in Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]) and in the Quarter Final ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]) in the [[Challenge Cup]].

Blythe featured in Round 17 Dewsbury  Round 23 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). Blythe played in Qualifier 1 ([[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]]) to Qualifier 4 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) then in Qualifier 7 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). Blythe played in the [[Million Pound Game|£1 Million Game]] ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). He scored against the [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] side (2 tries), the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] (1 try), [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] (3 tries) and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (1 try). However, the Bradford side were relegated to the [[Kingstone Press Championship]] by Wakefield Trinity club in the £1 Million Game.

Blythe featured in both pre-season friendlies against Leeds and [[Castleford Tigers]] before the [[2016 Bradford Bulls season]].

He featured in Round 1 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 4 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]) then in Round 6 ([[Batley Bulldogs|Bailey]]) to Round 7 ([[London Broncos]]). Blythe played in round 13 ([[Swinton Lions]]) to round 15 ([[Leigh Centurions]]) then in round 19 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]) to round 21 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]). He played in round 23 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]). Blythe featured in the [[Championship Shield]] Game 1 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]) to Game 2 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). Blythe played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the fourth round Dewsbury.

===Return to Warrington Wolves===
At the end of the season Blythe signed a one-year deal with former club [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37133272|title=Matty Blythe: Warrington Wolves re-sign Bradford Bulls utility man|work=BBC Sport|date=19 August 2016|access-date=20 February 2017}}</ref> He managed to get three games in 2017's [[Super League XXII]], against [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], all three games were lost. Blythe was loaned out to [[Rochdale Hornets]] of the [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]] on [[Rugby League Dual registration|dual registration]] where he played twice against [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]]. He announced his retirement having been released by Warrington outfit.
